_meta.purpose: Conservative triage of the 95 corpus claims left 'unassigned' by build_frontier_map's overrides+heuristics (ROADMAP.md §7 item 6). Applied by build_frontier_map.py ONLY where classify() returns 'unassigned' (never overrides a corpus/heuristic/verified call); confidence tagged 'triage'.
_meta.provenance: workflow run wf_1e87672b-249 (frontier-triage): 6 batch classifiers over the 95 unassigned claims + an adversarial skeptic pass on every verified/partial/tractable call. Result: 0 optimistic classifications survived scrutiny (none were proposed) — every claim is blocked (a genuine math statement needing a missing foundation) or informal (prose / attack-assessment / heuristic / method description). Conservatism rule: informal < blocked < tractable < partial < verified; ties break to the weaker bucket.
_meta.note: Only fills the unassigned gap. 'heights' and 'elliptic_nets' are new foundation keys (canonical/Neron-Tate heights + Mordell-Weil / point lifting; and general rank>=2 elliptic nets) — distinct confirmed barriers per BARRIERS.md, not curve_depth.

bv-hnp-006.reason: Heuristic dependency remark on multiplier distribution and approximation accuracy.

bv-hnp-008.reason: Method-requirement remark (needs auxiliary info beyond G and Q); prose, not formal.

bv-hnp-010.reason: Scope caveat that the theorem excludes arbitrary noisy/adversarial leakage; not a math statement.

hgs-lattice-001.reason: Description of the attack target (DSA-like signatures with known partial nonce bits).

hgs-lattice-002.reason: Scoping remark: attack operates on signature equations, not a single ECDLP instance.

hgs-lattice-003.reason: Prose describing that known contiguous nonce bits bound unknown fragments; leakage-model description, no formal statement.

hgs-lattice-004.reason: Method description: transforming signature equations into small-unknown congruences (HNP setup).

hgs-lattice-010.reason: Empirical feasibility observation about small leakage fractions in an implementation.

hgs-lattice-012.reason: Assumption description (many signatures per key, informative per-nonce leakage); not formal.

hgs-lattice-013.reason: Open-problem framing: no generic sub-Pollard ECDLP algorithm without leakage; a negative meta-remark, not a formal claim.

ladderleak-001.reason: Prose description of a side-channel leakage behavior (MSB of nonce leaks probabilistically); not a formal math statement.

ladderleak-002.reason: Qualitative description of leakage magnitude (<1 reliable bit/signature); implementation-attack prose, not formalizable content.

ladderleak-007.reason: Empirical demonstration report (key recovery on sect163r1/P-192); an attack assessment, not a formal statement.

ladderleak-010.reason: Scope/method-independence remark (weakness is implementation, not abstract ECDLP); prose.

ladderleak-012.reason: Prose about data requirements (many signatures per key); not a formal math statement.

minerva-001.reason: Attack description (timing leakage of ECDSA nonce bit-length in libraries/smartcard); prose.

minerva-003.reason: Empirical recovery report on a 256-bit curve; attack assessment, not a formal statement.

minerva-005.reason: Prose noting signature-count varies with leakage quality/noise; heuristic evaluation, not formalizable.

minerva-007.reason: Empirical comparison of signature counts (~40000 to ~900); measurement report, not a formal statement.

minerva-010.reason: Attack-assessment remark (deterministic nonces don't remove timing leakage); prose.

minerva-011.reason: Informational description of how each nonce bit-length adds key information; heuristic prose.

ns-ecdsa-001.reason: Core HNP claim: partial nonce leakage enables key recovery — a genuine math statement requiring lattice-reduction (LLL/BKZ, CVP) absent from Mathlib per BARRIERS B2.

ns-ecdsa-002.reason: Describes what the work studies (ECDSA modular relations from signatures/nonce bits); framing prose, not a formal statement.

ns-ecdsa-003.reason: Prose noting the analysis distinguishes MSB vs LSB leakage; descriptive, not a formal statement.

ns-ecdsa-005.reason: Prose about dependence on sufficiently many signatures; qualifier remark, not formalizable.

ns-ecdsa-006.reason: States nonce-independence and distributional model are assumptions; meta/assumption remark, not a formal statement.

ns-ecdsa-008.reason: Prose clarification of the ECDSA key-recovery threat model (key recovery from signatures + side info, not from G,Q alone); a scoping remark, not a formal statement.

ns-ecdsa-010.reason: Qualitative heuristic ('incorrect leakage can violate recovery conditions'); an attack-assessment remark with no crisp mathematical content.

ns-ecdsa-011.reason: Vague dependency statement ('sample size depends on leakage amount and bit position'); no formalized bound, so informal by the conservatism rule.

vow-distinguished-points-001.reason: Distinguished-point method with expected extra work O(1/theta) per trail; an expected-running-time complexity claim needing an oracle/operation-count framework absent from Mathlib.

vow-naive-parallel-002.reason: Theta(sqrt(m)) parallel-speedup / total-work claim for independent rho walks; a running-time statement requiring a cost model Lean lacks (BARRIERS B1).

vow-work-per-processor-004.reason: Exact expected work sqrt(pi*n/2)/m per processor plus overhead; a quantitative Theta running-time claim with no Mathlib operation-count framework.

cheon-optimal-divisor-exponents-008.reason: Cheon-attack complexity exponents (p^{1/4}, p^{1/3}) from minimizing cost bounds over divisors d; a complexity statement requiring a cost model.

ghs-transfer-map-dlp-006.reason: GHS/Weil-descent transfer of the EC relation to a DLP in the Jacobian (divisor class group) of the descended curve; needs higher-genus curve / divisor-class-group theory not in Mathlib. Closest fixed foundation is curve_depth; may warrant a new 'jacobian_divisor_class'.

smart-logarithm-quotient-formula-003.reason: Smart attack on anomalous curves: log_P(Q) = psi([p]Q)/psi([p]P) via the p-adic elliptic (formal-group) logarithm; needs formal-group/p-adic-log theory absent from Mathlib. Repo's anti-Smart result only proves secp256k1 is non-anomalous, not this attack formula. Closest fixed foundation curve_depth; may warrant new 'formal_group_padic_log'.

smart-padic-lift-reduction-002.reason: Smart method: lift to E(Q_p), multiply by p into the formal-group kernel, apply p-adic elliptic logarithm for a Z_p-linear relation; requires p-adic formal-group logarithm theory not in Mathlib. Closest curve_depth; possible new 'formal_group_padic_log'.

cheng-huang-shifting-prime-definition-00.reason: Explicitly a problem_definition (the 'shifting-prime problem'); a definitional framing, not an asserted theorem.

lauter-stange-net-zero-linear-relation-0.reason: Net-polynomial term vanishes iff sum v_i P_i = O (elliptic nets); elliptic-net theory (a multi-index generalization of division polynomials) is not in Mathlib. Closest fixed foundation curve_depth; may warrant new 'elliptic_nets'.

stange-zero-iff-point-relation-009.reason: Nondegenerate-elliptic-net term vanishes iff the indexed integer combination of points is O; requires elliptic-net theory absent from Mathlib. Closest curve_depth; possible new 'elliptic_nets'.

gaudry-small-degree-limitation-008.reason: Attack-applicability assessment (hidden constant grows with extension degree, so relevant only to small-degree fields); a heuristic scoping remark with no formal_statement.

semaev-binary-analogue-007.reason: Prose description of a characteristic-two proposal (low-degree F2 polynomials with a summation-polynomial congruence); a method description with no formal_statement.

survey-ffd-lfd-006.reason: Survey commentary comparing first-fall-degree vs last-fall-degree assumptions; a heuristic evaluation of proof-technique quality, not a formal statement.

survey-invariants-not-competitive-003.reason: Empirical attack-assessment: cited binary-field symmetry+invariant algorithms 'reported as not competitive with Pollard rho' — a performance/no-go remark, not a formal statement.

fppr2012-multihomogeneous-002.reason: Prose describing an algebraic-attack method (multihomogeneous block structure, Sub-Macaulay equation generation); a method description, not a crisp theorem.

gg2014-symmetry-breaking-dreg-006.reason: Empirical observation ('often had degree of regularity 3... reason was unclear') from experiments — a heuristic evaluation, not a formal statement.

ky2015-dreg-growth-005.reason: Experimental measurement (first fall degree fixed at 2, degree of regularity rising, 38 GB memory) — empirical benchmark data, not a theorem.

ky2015-trace-morphism-003.reason: Genuine math statement (trace on x-coord as surjective morphism to F2 giving a linear relation in the descended ideal) but tied to Semaev/descended-ideal machinery absent from Mathlib.

semaev2015-splitting-001.reason: Algorithm description: replacing one high-degree summation equation with a chain of third-summation equations via intermediate variables — a method, not a formal claim.

st2013-subfield-basis-005.reason: Experimental result (subfield basis gave 'extremely large speedups' and degree of regularity 3 for tested systems) — heuristic/empirical, not a theorem.

f4-minimum-degree-selection-008.reason: Describes F4 critical-pair selection heuristic as 'crucial for performance' — an algorithm/method description, not a formal statement.

jacobson-xedni-theorem4-success-bound-00.reason: Per-trial success probability bounded by const/p under height/discriminant assumptions; needs canonical-height + counting of rational lifts machinery absent in Mathlib (new foundation, flagged).

lauter-stange-perfect-periodic-point-com.reason: Computing perfectly-periodic net value at n from [n]P; requires Stange elliptic-net theory (beyond rank-1 normEDS in Mathlib), not present (new foundation, flagged).

stange-arbitrary-field-net-polynomials-0.reason: Net-polynomial construction over arbitrary base fields; a real construction absent from Mathlib (only rank-1 EDS exists), so blocked not informal (new foundation, flagged).

stange-degenerate-cases-open-014.reason: claim_type open_problem; explicitly frames degenerate/singular net cases as left to further work.

stange-finite-initial-determination-004.reason: Finite initial values determine rank-one/two/higher elliptic nets via recurrence; general multi-index (rank>=2) elliptic nets are absent from Mathlib per BARRIERS (new foundation, flagged).

stange-general-reconstruction-theorem-01.reason: Stange reconstruction: normalized nondegenerate net determines a cubic + marked points up to change of variables; deep general-elliptic-net theorem not in Mathlib (new foundation, flagged).

stange-laurentness-integrality-005.reason: Genuine theorem (Laurent property of elliptic-net recurrence -> integrality) but rank>=2 elliptic nets / net polynomials are absent from Mathlib; BARRIERS notes only rank-1 normEDS exists. Proposing NEW foundation 'elliptic_nets' (adjacent to curve_depth).

stange-net-polynomial-linear-relation-00.reason: Real math (net polynomials as rational functions on E^n whose zeros encode integer linear relations) but multi-index elliptic nets not in Mathlib. Proposing NEW foundation 'elliptic_nets' (flagged).

stange-unique-net-polynomial-family-008.reason: Uniqueness theorem for the normalized net-polynomial family; requires the missing elliptic-nets construction (rank>=2). Proposing NEW foundation 'elliptic_nets' (flagged).

pz-prime-field-scope-002.reason: Scope statement (GF(p) only, no binary/extension fields); prose.

pz-reversible-eea-011.reason: Method description of reversible extended-Euclidean circuit organization; not a formal statement.

pz-unique-point-representation-006.reason: Implementation-design remark on unique affine coordinate representation and omitting point-at-infinity; prose.

rns-binary-gcd-inversion-005.reason: Method description of reversible Montgomery inverse via binary extended Euclid; not a formal statement.

rns-exceptional-addition-boundary-017.reason: Design assessment of affine addition exceptional-case handling vs complete addition law; commentary, not a theorem.

rns-simulated-standard-curves-008.reason: Empirical claim about classical simulation/testing of point-addition networks for 192-521 bit curves; experimental, not formalizable.
